b:T7bd9,<div class="specification">
<p>Blue fin tuna (<em>Thunnus orientalis</em>) are top carnivores in the marine ecosystem. Some radioisotopes of elements are produced by nuclear reactors. Following a nuclear accident at Fukushima in Japan in March 2011, the levels of certain radioisotopes were measured in the bodies of blue fin tuna caught off California in August 2011, the other side of the Pacific Ocean from Japan. The becquerel (Bq) is a measure of radioactivity.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: reproduced from Pacific bluefin tuna transport Fukushima-derived radionuclides from Japan to California<br/>Daniel J. Madigan, Zofi a Baumann, and Nicholas S. Fisher <em>PNAS</em> June 12, 2012 <strong>109</strong> (24) 9483–9486]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Explain how the levels of the Caesium (Cs) radioisotopes could have accumulated in the tissues of these fish.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p style="text-align: left;">Describe the use of indicator species in monitoring environmental change.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>c:T52d,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. radioisotopes entered the sea/found in the water «in Japan» ✔</p>
<p>b. taken up by producers/phytoplankton/enter gills of fish ✔</p>
<p>c. producers eaten by consumers/fish ✔</p>
<p>d. «radioisotopes» are passed on up the food chain<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>contaminated fish are eaten by tuna /other fish ✔</p>
<p>e. radioisotopes are not easily excreted ✔</p>
<p>f. at each level there is bioaccumulation/biomagnification ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. presence/absence indicate environmental conditions ✔</p>
<p>b. changes in the environment affect these species ✔ <em>Number or type of species</em></p>
<p>c. «relative» numbers of individuals/indicator species can be used to calculate a biotic index ✔</p>
<p>d. changes monitored over time ✔ <em>Example of time frame</em></p>
<p>e. changes can lead to measures to protect the environment ✔</p>
<p>f. example of an indicator species <em><strong>AND</strong></em> what it indicates <em>«e.g.</em> <em>Tubifex for sediment pollution»</em> ✔ <em>Award marks for a species, not a group</em></p>
<p><em>Award mp for species not group</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/c/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","c"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19N.3.SL.TZ0.14"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","c-3-impacts-of-humans-on-ecosystems"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
